---
title: "LRPDID07: Waitlist-crossover (DiD) period-resolved session-dose response on letter-sound knowledge (L)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPDID07 is the **period-resolved session-dose response** on letter-sound
knowledge (YARC-LSK, `yarclet`) - the letter-sound analogue of LRPDID06's
word-reading dose model, with the dose slope **resolved by period**. It reuses
LRPDID02's identification (the phase-stacked waitlist-crossover frame, each child
their own control, the immediate arm anchoring the time/maturation trend) and
enters the standardised per-period intervention-session count with
**partial-pooled per-period slopes** `beta_dose_phase[p] = mu_dose + sigma_dose *
z_p`.

::: {.callout-note title="The question this answers"}

Does the dose-gain slope vary by period? The answer is **not** read off this
single fit - it is the **nested PSIS-LOO** of LRPDID07 (period-varying) against
LRPDID07base (pooled dose) in `did_dose_loo_compare.csv`. The word-reading
analogue (LRP77) found the slope **constant across periods** (LOO preferred the
pooled model), so the most likely outcome here is "no period variation at this n"
- itself a useful negative result.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="What this estimand is, and is not"}

The per-period session count is the **exposure** (`IS`): this is the **ID-3
observational dose-response**, **confounded by GA -> IS**, and must be read as an
*adjusted association* - never "more sessions cause more gain". The DiD machinery
adjusts only `{IG, A}` and **never conditions on cumulative sessions**
(`attend_cumul`, the `IS` collider) - the conditioning that closed PR #108. Only
the randomised contrast (the ITT LRPITT07, or the DiD arm term LRPDID02) is causal.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Sign convention and the ceiling"}

Positive => the intervention helps. The Beta-Binomial **logit** link absorbs
ceiling effects: an immediate-arm child near the top of a bounded test makes a
small P2 gain *expected*, not a spurious negative time trend - which is exactly the
failure mode of a raw-gain DiD on the count scale.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_{i,p} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period}}\,[p = \text{P2}]
           + \beta_{\text{dose},p}\,z(\text{sessions}_{i,p})
           + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(L_{\mathrm{pre},i,p}, N)
           + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i,p} + u_{\text{child},i},
$$

with $\beta_{\text{dose},p} = \mu_{\text{dose}} + \sigma_{\text{dose}}\,z_p$
(non-centred, partial-pooled across periods) and a Beta-Binomial likelihood on the
period post-count, $N = 32$. `sessions` is the standardised per-period
intervention-session count. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_did.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
